item.title: CID : Captioning for Interpretability and Disentanglement
item.description: This is an ongoing research project. For further details, please contact me.

item.title: HRHQ : High Resolution High Quality image generation and disentanglement
item.description: This is an ongoing research project. For further details, please contact me.

item.title: RUDE : 3d Reconstruction Using DisEntanglement
item.description: This is an ongoing research project. For further details, please contact me.

item.title: Unsupervised Domain Adaption for cross domain Lung Segmentation on Chest Xray Images
item.description: This is an ongoing research project. For further details, please contact me.

item.title: COVID-SWIFT : Simple Whatsapp based x-ray Image interpretation at your Finger Tips for potential COVID19 patients
DSLR : Dynamic to Static LiDAR scan Reconstruction using adversarially trained autoencoder
Why do we need generative models?
item.description: In this talk, we briefly survey the existing generative models used for different tasks and applications and highlight few open problems. This was presented as an unconference session in EEML summer school 2020.

item.title: COVID19 Image Segmentation
item.description: In this talk, we briefly present how we can use probabilistic U-Nets for disease segmentation on X-Ray and CT images of potential COVID-19 patients. This was presented as an unconference session in EEML summer school 2020.

item.title: Continual Learning for Sequential Data Streaming
item.description: In this talk, we briefly present how we can use continual learning for sequential online data without catastrophic forgetting. This was presented as an unconference session in EEML summer school 2020.

item.title: An Approach For Accurate Sceneflow Prediction For LiDAR-based Sensors.
Cartographer - Real-Time Loop Closure in 2D LIDAR SLAM
item.description: In this talk, I review the paper Real-Time Loop Closure in 2D LIDAR SLAM. In a prior video, I have also explained the basics of SLAM and gave intuitions required to understand the novel ideas proposed in this paper. The code for Cartographer paper is open sourced here and widely used and deployed in self driving community for performing accurate LIDAR based SLAM.

item.title: SLAM Basics
item.description: In this talk, I explain the basics of SLAM and give intuition of novel ideas proposed in the paper Real-Time Loop Closure in 2D LIDAR SLAM. In next video, I review the paper in detail.

item.title: Reviewing Multi Task Learning
item.description: Literature review of Multi Task Learning.

item.title: FAIR : Frugal ADAS for Indian Roads
Introduction to Deep Learning
item.description: In this talk, I discuss why we are seeing incredible success in using deep learning based methods and what are some of its notable applications.

item.title: Understanding Hector SLAM
item.description: Paper review of Hector SLAM paper.

item.title: Deep Learning in (visual) SLAM
item.description: Literature survey of use of deep learning for visual SLAM applications.

item.title: Residual blocks — Building blocks of ResNet
Separable convolutions — trading little accuracy for huge computational gains
Grouped Convolutions — convolutions in parallel
Deciding optimal kernel size for CNN
Depth Map for Image Classification
item.description:
• Improving image classification using depth maps
  • An end-to-end Google colab tutorial project

item.title: Smart Basket Prediction
item.description:
• Using past orders data, predict which previously purchased products will be in a user’s next order.
  • Trained on relevant engineered features based on users and items
  • Third best performing model on the dataset

item.title: Deep Traffic for self driving car
item.description:
• Experimented with different models for deep Q-learning network
  • Received an award for finishing with top finalists

item.title: Global NIPS Paper Implementation Challenge
item.description:
My primary motivation to implement this paper “Thy Friend Is My Friend: Iterative Collaborative Filtering for Sparse Matrix Estimation” was to understand how recommendation systems work. The paper talks about a new way of user-item prediction in recommender systems for extremely sparse datasets. After reading the dataset, we make sure the dataset has been made symmetric and the values are normalised. In step 1, we divide the user-item rating pairs randomly into three parts and use each part in next 3 steps. In step 2, we take first part and calculate neighbourhood vector for every vertex i.e. get the product along the shortest path from given vertex to every possible vertex. In step 3, we use part 2 obtained and use it to calculate distance between any two vertices (like user and movie) as an inner product between r-hop and r+1 hop neighbourhood vector. In step 4, we average over all vertices within a distance threshold to predict user-item rating.

To test validity of the code, we experimentally showed that for recommendation dataset,

if $$p > 1/n$$ where $p$ is sparsity and $n$ is dimension of the matrix,

then Mean Square Error of the algorithm in the paper (and our implementation) is bounded by $$MSE < O((pn)^{-1/5})$$

item.title: Text Normalization for English
item.description:
• Convert English text from written expressions into spoken forms
  • Obtained 99.637% prediction accuracy using xgboost and std. text cleaning

item.title: Car Image Masking
item.description:
• Automatically identify the boundaries of the car in an image
  • Implemented U net in keras and whose mean dice coefficient was 0.9963

item.title: Paraphrase detection on Quora questions
item.description:
• Identify question pairs that have the same intent
  • Tried LSTM but got 0.489 log loss error using hand tuned features and parameters trained on logistic regression with regularization.

item.title: Hierarchical Task Mapping on Dragonfly topology for scaling Molecular Dynamics
Establishing Semantic relationships among object classes using Deep Networks to Image Classification
